description: An introduction to the design of engineering structures which control and/or utilize runoff, emphasizing the sizing of structures to meet hydrologic uncertainty. Applies principles and techniques from several disciplines, including hydrology, hydraulics, probability and statistics. Specific techniques include flood frequency analysis; risk analysis; design storm and historic storm techniques; rainfall-runoff modeling.

prerequisites.prerequisites_text: G L E/​CIV ENGR  291andCIV ENGR 311, graduate/professional standing, or member of Engineering Guest Students
